coordination_patterns.why_it_scales: future sessions can consume reviewed compact artifacts instead of re-reading raw transcripts or private context
safe claims and blocked claims travel together, so handoffs can move fast without accidental promotion
reviewed municipal learning can improve dispatch prep while the system still blocks direct worker-copyable doctrine
Acontext should carry already-reviewed meaning; it should not become the place where future agents strengthen claims

decision_basis: live sink writes are still false
decision_support.highest_value_connection: reviewed municipal proof blocks can become the common handoff language between memory, IRC-style coordination, dispatch, and Acontext transport

multiplier_effects.multiplier: one reviewed redirect can change many future dispatches
multiplier_effects.requires_next: prove live transport parity before increasing surface area

multiplier_effects.multiplier: other agent systems can consume bounded verdicts, not raw private municipal conversations

scaling_rules.reason: coordination_session_id, compact_decision_id, review_packet_id, and proof_anchor_id survive handoffs better than prose summaries
raw uploads and unreviewed memory can inform review but cannot become durable municipal doctrine
fast agent coordination is safest when every handoff includes what not to say yet
a polished console over unproven live retrieval would hide the most important risk
one redirect proof can justify cautious operator guidance, not general worker-copyable city doctrine
